To a stirred solution of salicylic acid (1.38 g, 10.0 mmol, 1.0 equiv) in acetic anhydride (5.0 mL) was added concentrated sulfuric acid (3 drops, catalytic) at room temperature. The mixture was warmed to 85 °C and stirred for 30 min, then cooled to 0 °C with an ice bath. Cold water (20 mL) was added to quench the reaction, and the resulting suspension was stirred for 10 min. The precipitate was collected by filtration, washed with cold water (3 x 10 mL), and dried under vacuum to afford acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) as a white crystalline solid (1.62 g, 90% yield).
